style_guide.art_style: Textured digital illustration with visible paint-like brushstrokes and detailed line work, reminiscent of classic nature guides, giving depth and a handcrafted feel.
style_guide.color_palette: Deep jungle greens, earthy ochres and rusts, vibrant sky blue, and warm sunset oranges, with rich purples and blues for deep shadows.
style_guide.lighting: Warm, bright daylight filtering through thick leaves, creating speckled shadows and golden highlights, enhancing textures.

characters.name: Mia
characters.description: A girl, approximately 10 years old, with warm golden-brown skin and short curly auburn hair that springs around her face. She has wide, inquisitive hazel eyes and a sprinkle of freckles across her nose. She is slender and agile. She wears a sturdy, multi-pocket khaki explorer's vest over a forest-green long-sleeved t-shirt, dark brown cargo trousers with reinforced knees, and rugged red hiking boots with thick soles. A vintage brass compass hangs from a leather cord around her neck, and a small magnifying glass is clipped to her belt.

pages.narration: Mia wiped sweat from her forehead as the hot sun beat down on the dry, dusty excavation site. In front of her, something huge was peeking out of the orange rock. It wasn't a rock; it was a fossilized bone! A whole team of paleontologists, scientists who study ancient life, worked nearby using small brushes and tools to carefully uncover incredible secrets from the past.

visual.setting: Dry, dusty paleontology dig site with orange earth and excavation tools
visual.action: Mia is carefully cleaning a massive dinosaur bone in the ground

pages.narration: The bone was absolutely enormous, bigger than Mia! With careful strokes of her brush, she revealed more of the massive, stony thigh bone. These incredible creatures lived during the Mesozoic Era, which was a time of colossal dinosaurs and lush jungles. This particular bone belonged to a giant from the Jurassic Period, a time millions of years ago.

visual.action: Mia touches the stone-like texture of the enormous uncovered bone

pages.narration: It was a *Diplodocus*, one of the longest land animals that ever walked the Earth! They were herbivores, which means they ate plants, probably using their incredibly long necks to reach high tree branches. Just imagine a creature that big eating only leaves and ferns all day long. How did they find enough food and protect themselves? Mia needed more clues to understand how this gentle giant lived.

visual.time_of_day: Golden late afternoon sun through museum windows

choice.question: What clues about this dinosaur's life should Mia explore next?

option_a.label: Follow its footprints in fossilized mud.
option_a.image_prompt: Textured digital illustration showing large dinosaur tracks pressed into cracked mud near a waterhole under a cloudy sky.

pages.narration: Mia arrived at a massive rock slab filled with deep, preserved dinosaur footprints. It looked like an ancient muddy plain that had turned into solid stone. Scientists can determine how fast a dinosaur moved, its weight, and even if it ran or walked by studying these trackways. She found two parallel sets of large tracks, revealing something surprising.
pages.image_prompt: Textured digital illustration with visible paint-like brushstrokes and detailed line work. Mia, a girl, approximately 10 years old, with warm golden-brown skin and short curly auburn hair that springs around her face. She has wide, inquisitive hazel eyes and a sprinkle of freckles across her nose. She is slender and agile. She wears a sturdy, multi-pocket khaki explorer's vest over a forest-green long-sleeved t-shirt, dark brown cargo trousers with reinforced knees, and rugged red hiking boots with thick soles. A vintage brass compass hangs from a leather cord around her neck, and a small magnifying glass is clipped to her belt. She is kneeling and examining deep, rounded dinosaur tracks impressed in a large, tilted slab of sandstone rock. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: Exposed rock face with multiple sets of deep tracks under bright sun
visual.action: Mia is carefully measuring and studying the tracks

pages.narration: The trackways showed a clear pattern: multiple sets of prints moving in the exact same direction, side-by-side. Some tracks were smaller, and some were much larger, like a whole family was travelling together! This discovery was proof that *Diplodocus* lived in herds, probably migrating together for food and protection. Think of migrating wildebeest or elephants on a long journey!

option_b.label: Look for teeth marks and dung clues.
option_b.image_prompt: Textured digital illustration showing scattered bones and a few distinctive shark-like teeth on sandy ground, with dried coprolites nearby.

pages.narration: Mia began searching for clues on the fossils themselves, looking for evidence of other animals. Carnivores, meat-eating dinosaurs with sharp teeth, often left distinct bite marks or even teeth embedded in the bones of their prey or carcasses. Sometimes paleontology reveals the dangers of the ancient world! She carefully scanned a *Stegosaurus* bone and spotted something strange.

visual.setting: Paleontology workbench with tools and bones
visual.action: Mia is looking at bite marks on a bone through a magnifying glass

pages.narration: To confirm what the dinosaur ate, Mia examined an unusual fossil: coprolite, which is fossilized dung! Poop can contain parts of plants, bones, and even other fossils, telling scientists exactly what was on the dinosaur’s menu. Analyzing these unique fossils is another way to determine if a dinosaur was a herbivore, carnivore, or omnivore. This coprolite had tiny teeth and fish scales in it! A carnivore!

pages.narration: Whether it's finding monstrous footprints in mud, examining ancient bite marks, or analyzing dinosaur dung, every discovery adds another incredible piece to the prehistoric puzzle. Each fossil tells a fascinating story about a lost world from millions of years ago, when giants roamed the Earth. Mia can't wait to grab her brush and see what incredible secrets the very next clue might reveal. Keep exploring!
